content: After thirty-eight years ranching outside Cody and two terms on this board, I've come around to the consolidation plan. Keeping four half-empty schoolhouses open is bleeding the budget that should be paying teachers. Sharing the load across District 3 is how we keep good teachers and keep the lights on.
excerpt: Why a lifelong rancher came around to consolidating District 3's schools.
title: Why I support consolidating our rural schools

content: Plenty of rumors going around, so here is what the proposal actually says: consolidate the three smallest District 3 schools into the Powell campus starting fall 2027, keep the Cody elementary open, and reroute buses. No school closes before a full year of public hearings. Read the full draft on the About the Proposal page.
excerpt: What the consolidation proposal actually says, minus the rumors.
title: What the consolidation proposal actually says

content: The number families ask me about most is time on the bus. Under the draft routing, no District 3 child rides more than fifty-five minutes one way, and most ride less than they do today. The Bus Route Impact page has the stop-by-stop maps. If your road isn't on there, write me.
excerpt: A plain look at bus times for District 3 kids under the new routing.
title: Bus routes and time on the road for District 3 kids

content: Letter as submitted to the Cody Enterprise. Doing nothing has a price too. Heating four aging buildings and busing teachers between them costs the district more every winter. The money runs out whether we vote or not. I'd rather we decide than have the state decide for us.
excerpt: Op-ed submitted to the Cody Enterprise on the cost of doing nothing.
title: Letter to the Cody Enterprise: the cost of doing nothing

content: About forty families turned out to the District 3 town hall at the Powell grange. Good questions on bus stops, on what happens to the Clark building, and on teacher pay. I took notes and the board will answer each in writing. Thank you to everyone who drove in on a cold night.
excerpt: Notes from the District 3 town hall at the Powell grange.
title: Field notes from the District 3 town hall

content: The board votes on the consolidation plan this spring. Here is how District 3 families can weigh in: sign up to speak at a hearing, send a letter to the board, or just talk to your neighbors. The How to Help page has the hearing dates and the sign-up form.
excerpt: How District 3 families can weigh in before the spring vote.
title: How District 3 families can weigh in
